_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that a complex sentence has one main part (independent clause) and at least one extra part (dependent clause) joined by words like 'because', 'when', 'although' or 'if'.

_source.w: We are learning that a complex sentence has one main part (independent clause) and at least one extra part (dependent clause) joined by words like 'because', 'when', 'although' or 'if'.
y: Complex sentences let us express more detailed ideas and show relationships between them.
Using complex sentences makes our writing more mature and nuanced.
Understanding how clauses connect helps us read and write more effectively.
eg: We identify the independent clause and dependent clause in a complex sentence.
We use 'because' to add a reason: 'We stayed inside because it was raining.'
We use 'although' to show contrast: 'Although it was cold, we still went outside.'
We combine simple sentences into complex sentences using subordinating conjunctions.
We find complex sentences in our reading and identify the joining word.
We practise writing complex sentences that begin with the dependent clause.
